title: Trade in services as share of GDP
subtitle: Total services trade (exports and imports) divided by [gross domestic product](#dod:gdp) (GDP).
caption:
Trade in services as share of GDP

Total services trade (exports and imports) divided by gross domestic product (GDP).

This line chart tracks the share of total services trade in GDP from 1970 to 2024 across seven regional groupings and a global average. The vertical axis ranges from 0% to 30%. The line for Europe and Central Asia begins near 9% in the early 1970s and rises steadily, crossing 20% around 2010 and reaching approximately 28% by 2024, the highest value on the chart. The line for MENA, Afghanistan and Pakistan starts near 14% in the late 1970s, fluctuates between 12% and 17% through the 1990s and 2000s, then climbs sharply after 2020 to about 23%. The World average line begins below 6% in 1970 and rises gradually to roughly 15% by 2024. East Asia and Pacific and Sub-Saharan Africa both start below 5% in the 1970s; East Asia and Pacific rises to about 11% by 2024, while Sub-Saharan Africa reaches approximately 10%. Latin America and Caribbean begins near 4% and ends around 8%, and North America starts near 3% and ends near 7%. All lines show an upward trend over the full period, with varying rates of increase and periods of fluctuation. The gap between the highest and lowest lines widens over time, particularly after 2000.
